title: Boat Rental in Cuba Havana: Costs, Routes and Tips for 2026
topic: things to do by boat in Cuba Havana
html:
Exploring Cuba Havana by boat lets you discover secluded coves and vibrant coastlines. Most trips depart from Marina Hemingway, with half-day motorboat hire starting around EUR 250. If you want to compare verified boat listings, you can book a charter online through BoatHire24.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Cuba Havana?

Prices generally range from EUR 150 for a shared tour to EUR 1,200 for a crewed yacht day charter. Bareboat motorboats of 18, 24 feet typically cost EUR 250, 400 for a half-day, while a full-day catamaran for up to 12 guests might be EUR 700, 1,000. Larger crewed yachts, such as a Sunseeker or Azimut, can exceed EUR 1,500 per day. Always confirm fuel and crew charges separately because they can add roughly EUR 100 to the total. The booking FAQ explains payment and cancellation policies in more detail. No licence is needed for small powerboats, but the operator will confirm exact requirements at the time of booking. You can book a charter online in a few minutes.

Where to set off in Cuba Havana

The best starting points include Marina Hemingway, Marina Tarará, and the port of Havana Harbour. Marina Hemingway, west of the city, is the main hub for charter boats and offers deep-water access. Marina Tarará, east of Havana, is quieter and ideal for sailing towards the Playas del Este. From Havana Harbour you can depart for a tour of the bay, passing El Morro fortress. For longer trips, head to the nearby town of Jaimanitas or the island of Cayo Levisa, roughly 40 nautical miles west. Each departure point has its own character and proximity to anchorages. One option worth a look is the Olympic 490 for hire.

Which boat to rent in Cuba Havana

Choose a boat type based on your group size and desired activity. The following options are available through BoatHire24. You can also see more at check availability.

  1. Small motorboat, Best for couples or small groups exploring close coves; seats 4, 6 people; from EUR 250 half-day.
  2. Bowrider, Ideal for a leisurely afternoon with friends; seats 6, 8 guests; from EUR 350 half-day.
  3. Catamaran, Excellent for stable family cruising with space; seats up to 12; from EUR 700 full-day.
  4. Crewed yacht, Perfect for a luxurious day trip with a skipper; seats 8, 10; from EUR 900 full-day.
  5. Speedboat, Best for thrill-seekers wanting to reach Cayo Levisa quickly; seats 4, 6; from EUR 400 half-day. For a practical 5-metre option, see the Olympic 490 for hire on the platform.

When is the best season for boat rental?

The peak season runs from November to April, when the trade winds are most reliable. During these months you can expect steady breezes, calm seas, and blue skies. The local wind, known as the viento alisio, blows from the northeast at 10, 20 knots, making sailing conditions ideal. Summer months (May to October) bring higher humidity and the possibility of tropical storms, but also lower prices and fewer crowds. If you choose to visit in summer, morning trips are recommended to avoid afternoon squalls. To see how booking works, visit the booking FAQ.

Book your trip early in the day to enjoy flat water before the afternoon wind builds, especially near Marina Hemingway.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 150, 1,500+ per day
  • Peak months: November to April
  • Trip length: 3, 8 hours (half or full day)
  • Guest capacity: 4, 12 people per boat
  • Currency: EUR (quoted in euro, though local payment may be in CUP)

Questions about boat rental in Cuba Havana

Do I need a boating licence to rent a boat in Cuba Havana?

For bareboat rental of small motorboats (under 30 hp) you generally do not need a licence, but the operator will confirm exact requirements at the time of booking.

Can I rent a boat for a full day without a skipper?

Yes, many motorboats under 8 metres are available for bareboat charter, provided you are over 18 and show basic boating competence.

What is included in the rental price?

Most rentals include boat, safety equipment, and basic insurance; fuel, crew, and extras like snorkel gear are usually charged separately.

How far can I travel from Havana in a day trip?

A typical half-day trip covers about 10, 15 nautical miles, while a full-day trip can reach Cayo Levisa or the northern keys, around 40 nautical miles away.

Whether you are planning a relaxing family outing or a fishing adventure, renting a boat in Cuba Havana opens up a beautiful coastline. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Cuba Havana or list your own, and you can check availability on the platform for the best options. Owners interested in reaching international guests should consider listing their boat to benefit from increased visibility.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Cuba Havana and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
